substanceContext.plainEnglishMeaning: Cannabis DNA Health Report is represented as an evidence-boundary package. It can summarize supplied local evidence and references, while marking unsupported personal conclusions unavailable.
substanceContext.professionalReview: Use qualified professional review for medical, behavioral-health, medication, pregnancy, workplace, and legal decisions; this synthetic local result is not enough for those decisions.

professionalReview: Use this local result as educational context for cannabis and substance-response genetics, not as a decision tool.
Collect validated source output, source provenance, and relevant clinical or professional context before using Cannabis DNA Health Report for personal decisions.
Discuss medical, behavioral-health, medication, pregnancy, workplace, and legal decisions with qualified professionals when those choices matter.

validated cannabis-response model, product exposure, dose, route, age, mental-health context, medication context, and authenticated sample-report rows

Report unavailable cannabis genetics and substance-response context evidence explicitly; do not recommend cannabis, cannabis products, CBD, THC, dose, route, strain, timing, or use pattern; predict impairment, addiction, psychosis, anxiety, cognition, pregnancy outcome, interaction risk, safety, legal status, therapeutic benefit, adverse-event risk, or clinical actionability from genotype.
uncertainty: No validated cannabis and substance-response genetics model with source provenance and professional review is supplied for this local package.
The supplied rows support evidence-boundary education only and should not be treated as a personal prediction or clinical interpretation.
limitations: This validation result uses selected derived evidence only; raw genome data, VCF, FASTQ, BAM, CRAM, and complete callset files are not included.
The package does not recreate unavailable Sequencing.com authenticated report rows or proprietary third-party output for Cannabis DNA Health Report.
The package does not support: cannabis safety claim; THC or CBD dose guidance; product recommendation; substance-use risk estimate; mental-health or medication advice; legal or occupational advice.
